sections.paragraph:
περιβλέπω (Soph., X., Pla. et al.; BGU 1097, 3; LXX; Jos., Bell. 1, 627 al.) in our lit. only mid. περιβλέπομαι (Polyb.; Περὶ ὕψους 55, 18 V. al.; LXX; TestJos 7:1; cp. B-D-F §316, 1; Rob. 809; 813) impf. 3 sg. περιεβλέπετο; fut. 3 sg. περιβλέψεται Job 7:8; 1 aor. 3 sg. περιεβλέψατο 3 Km 21:40; ptc. περιβλεψάμενος.

① to glance at or look around in various directions, look around (at) abs. (Diod S 16, 32, 2; Plut., Cato Min. 777 [37; 8]; Ex 2:12; 3 Km 21:40) Mk 9:8; 10:23. W. final inf. foll. (B-D-F §392, 3; cp. Rob. 989 f) περιεβλέπετο ἰδεῖν he looked (or kept looking) around to see Mk 5:32. W. acc. of pers. (Polyb. 9, 17, 6; Job 7:8) περιβλεψάμενος τοὺς περὶ αὐτὸν κύκλῳ καθημένους he looked around at those etc. Mk 3:34. Cp. vs. 5; Lk 6:10. περιβλεψάμενος πάντα when he had looked around at everything Mk 11:11. περιέβλεπεν τὰ δεξιὰ καὶ τὰ ἀριστερά she looked to the right and to the left GJs 11:1. περιβλεψάμενος [γὰρ κύκλῳ ὁ λέων] AcPl Ha 4, 29.

② be on the lookout for, look for, hunt, w. loss of the literal mng. (w. acc. Epict. 3, 22, 65; M. Ant. 7, 55; Lucian, Vit. Auct. 12) περιβλέπονται τίνα ἐκδύσωσιν they are looking for someone to plunder B 10:4. Ἐλισάβεδ … περιεβλέπετο ποῦ αὐτὸν ἀποκρύψῃ Elizabeth looked about to see where she could hide (John) GJs 22:3.—M-M s.v. περιβλέπομαι.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

impf. impf. = imperfect

  1. sg. = singular

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

abs. abs. = absolute

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

acc. acc. = accusative

pers. pers. = person(s)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

AcPl AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 799
